title: Understanding Forces
text: To find the tension in the cable, consider the forces acting on the elevator. The forces include the gravitational force and the force due to acceleration.

title: Gravitational Force
text: The gravitational force acting on the elevator can be calculated using the formula: $$F_{gravity}=m g$$, where $$m$$ is the mass of the elevator and g is the acceleration due to gravity $$(9.8$$ m/s**2).

title: Calculate Gravitational Force
text: Calculate the gravitational force acting on the elevator using the formula $$F_{gravity}=m g$$.

title: Net Force
text: The net force acting on the elevator is the sum of the force due to acceleration and the gravitational force. Use the formula: $$F_{net}=m a$$, where a is the acceleration of the elevator.

title: Calculate Net Force
text: Calculate the net force acting on the elevator using the formula $$F_{net}=m a$$.

title: Tension in the Cable
text: The tension in the cable is equal to the sum of the gravitational force and the net force. Use the formula: $$Tension=F_{gravity}+F_{net}$$.

title: Calculate Tension
text: Calculate the tension in the cable using the formula $$Tension=F_{gravity}+F_{net}$$.
